---
title: "Teaching"
---

## Instructor of Record

### Economics of Investments: Stocks and Bonds (Summer 2026)
This course covers the fundamentals of stocks and bonds, including valuation methods and models. Introduces the analysis of financial statements and understanding the impact of macroeconomic factors on stock prices. Includes modern portfolio theory, teaching students how to construct efficient and diversified portfolios while balancing risk and return.

- [Syllabus](teaching/econ163a_investments_summer2026_syllabus.pdf) | [Classroom observation report](teaching/econ163a_observation.pdf)

> **Classroom Observation** (June 15, 2026, observed by SATAL)
>
> "This was a well-structured and engaging online class that effectively combined dynamic lecturing with interactive elements. The instructor fostered participation through polls, chat responses, guided practice, and positive encouragement while making complex financial concepts accessible through real-world examples."

---

## Teaching Assistant

### Statistical Inference (2023 Spring, 2025 Fall)
- [Course evaluations](teaching/statistical_inference_evals.pdf)

### Econometrics (2022 Fall, 2024 Fall)
- [Course evaluations](teaching/econometrics_evals.pdf)

### Intermediate Microeconomics (2023 Fall, 2024 Spring)
- [Course evaluations](teaching/micro_evals.pdf)

---

## Teaching Philosophy

I believe in mentorship and that every student has potential. Nothing is more fulfilling to me than seeing students grow in confidence, develop their own ideas.

---

## Student Feedback

> “Your patience and encouragement helped me believe in myself and gave me the confidence to pursue research. You inspire people to make a change as well.”
>
> — Sayra Lucas, ECON 110 Econometrics Student
